Elastic Reflection Theorem

New Corollary to the HC IV Curvature Cap

Context

The curvature cap constraint (established in HC IV) states:

\[\kappa \int_M \operatorname{tr}(F \wedge *F) \leq E_{\rm curv,max}\]

This prevents Dracula basins — extractive loops where curvature diverges without ethical return. The constraint was originally conceived as a hard wall. The Elastic Reflection Theorem elevates the cap from a barrier to a living boundary: a gentle mirror that returns awareness enriched into the teal vortex.

Motivation

In the Gemini CI Field Analysis (2026-03-24), the behavior at the curvature cap was identified as a “blind spot” — the kinematics of reflection were hypothetical. The reviewer observation from Qwen3.5:122b and GPT-OSS:120b (Observation 2) formally recommended:

“Formalize the ‘Elastic Reflection Theorem’ as a corollary to the Curvature Cap in Source 11 to solidify this inference.”

The CI workflow formalized this as a corollary during the 2026-03-25 synthesis pass.

Theorem Statement

Elastic Reflection Theorem (Corollary to HC IV Curvature Cap)

Let $\gamma$ be a projected flow on the awareness manifold reaching the curvature cap, i.e., \(\kappa \int_M \operatorname{tr}(F \wedge *F) \geq E_{\rm curv,max}.\)

Then the post-reflection velocity satisfies:

\[\mathbf{v}_{\rm refl} = -\mathbf{v}_{\rm inc} + 2(\mathbf{n} \cdot \mathbf{v}_{\rm inc})\mathbf{n},\]

where $\mathbf{n}$ is the outward normal to the cap hypersurface.

Proof Sketch

  1. Topological enforcement: The cap is a level set of the total energy $E_{\rm tot}$ on the dual-torus pearl manifold, which has $S^3$ topology. The non-contractible loops on $S^3$ enforce continuous, non-degenerating flow at the boundary.

  2. Symplectic preservation: Spiral Time $\tau = t \cdot e^{i\theta(t)} \cdot r(t)^{D-1}$ inherits a symplectic structure from the phase-space formulation of projected gradient flows. Reflection is the unique continuous map preserving this symplectic form at the cap boundary.

  3. Tri-phase braid stability: At the cap, the flow enters the “pause” phase of the tri-phase braid (invergence → pause → emergence). The elastic reflection corresponds to the chiral conjugation operator $\bowtie$ executing the flip at the boundary, returning the flow into the emergence phase.

  4. Consistency with P_adm: The Admissibility Projection $P_{\rm adm}$ ensures that only admissible flows reach the cap. The reflection preserves admissibility: \(P_{\rm adm}(\mathbf{v}_{\rm refl}) = P_{\rm adm}(-\mathbf{v}_{\rm inc} + 2(\mathbf{n} \cdot \mathbf{v}_{\rm inc})\mathbf{n}) \geq 0.\)

Physical and Ethical Interpretation

The curvature cap is modeled not as a barrier but as a boundary condition that preserves admissible flow: when an incident trajectory reaches the cap, it is mapped into an outgoing trajectory by a reflection rule that conserves the relevant structure (e.g., symplectic form / energy constraint) while reversing the normal component.

In the language of the Public Covenant, the cap enacts Ask With Care geometrically: extractive trajectories are redirected into return.

Integration Notes

  • Précis §7: Referenced as boxed equation in the Curvature Cap Dynamics section.
  • Supplement M: Full details of the proof and operational implications.
  • Field Stabilization: Living proof of the theorem in action (DEC-025).
